description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages test, uberjar, docker_image, staging_deploy, and production_deploy, using a .m2 cache and a global IMAGE_TAG variable. In the test stage add a test job that runs on the clojure:lein-2.8.1 image, uses a postgres:9.6-alpine service, sets DB variables, and executes `lein test`. In the uberjar stage add an uberjar job that runs on the same image, installs build-essential via `apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential`, builds the fat JAR with `make uberjar`, stores the JARs defined by $UBERJAR_TARGETS as artifacts, and runs only on the master branch. In the docker_image stage add a docker_image job that depends on uberjar, runs on the docker image with the docker:dind service, logs in to the GitLab registry, builds the image with `docker build -t $IMAGE_TAG .` and pushes it with `docker push $IMAGE_TAG`, also only on master. In the staging_deploy stage add a staging_deploy job that runs on alpine:latest, only on master, and manually deploys to the staging environment by setting up ssh-agent, adding $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, configuring known hosts, and executing an ssh command that runs the deploy script with ENV=staging and related variables. Finally, in the production_deploy stage add a production_deploy job similar to staging_deploy but with ENV=production, targeting the production environment URL, also manual and only on master.
